next_steps: Perform renal biopsy for histologic diagnosis
Check serum complement levels (C3, C4) and ANA/anti-dsDNA titers
Initiate supportive therapy with ACE inhibitors or ARBs for proteinuria
Consider empiric treatment with diuretics for symptomatic edema
Assess for hypercoagulability given nephrotic-range proteinuria
Evaluate subclinical hypothyroidism with thyroid peroxidase antibodies

differential_diagnoses: Preeclampsia superimposed on chronic glomerular disease
next_steps: Perform renal biopsy for definitive histopathologic diagnosis
Measure 24-hour urine protein excretion and creatinine clearance
Perform serum immunologic workup: ANA, anti-dsDNA, C3, C4, and serum anti-PLA2R antibody titers
Obtain renal ultrasound to assess for signs of chronic kidney disease or renal vein thrombosis
Initiate ACE inhibitor or ARB therapy for proteinuria control, monitoring serum creatinine and potassium
Evaluate for secondary causes of nephrotic syndrome, including viral serologies (HIV, HBV, HCV) and serum protein electrophoresis
